emails.subject: Off-Peak
emails.body: Off-Peak Kay- According to the control room guys, "off peak" is defined as hour ending 2300 to hour ending 0600. They indicate that this is a NERC standard.

emails.subject: Off-Peak
emails.body: Off-Peak Kay- According to the control room guys, "off peak" is defined as hour ending 2300 to hour ending 0600. They indicate that this is a NERC standard.

emails.subject: Re: Off-Peak
emails.body: Re: Off-Peak Every day? From: Reagan Rorschach/ENRON@enronXgate on 03/19/2001 03:51 PM

emails.subject: RE: Off-Peak
emails.body: RE: Off-Peak Kay- Off-peak includes all weekends and NERC defined holidays. For example, Friday at hour ending 2300 until hour ending 0600 Monday, you are off-peak. Let me know if you need those holidays, the control room guys can dig them up.

emails.subject: RE: Off-Peak
